subject: Biology
title: Inflammation: The Alarm Signal
description: A quick win: understand Inflammation: The Alarm Signal.

hook.text: Redness, swelling, heat, and pain at a wound aren't problems—they're your immune system working.

buildup.text: Damaged cells release chemical signals like histamine that trigger blood vessel dilation.

discovery.text: Widened vessels let more blood and immune cells flood the area—causing redness and warmth.

twist.text: Chronic inflammation—when the alarm never shuts off—drives heart disease, diabetes, and cancer.

climax.text: Anti-inflammatory drugs work by blocking these chemical signals, not by fighting infection.

punchline.text: Inflammation heals you—unless it won't stop.

quiz.question: What causes redness and warmth at a wound site?
options: Blood vessels widen, flooding the area with blood
